content.claim: The Hungarian education system contains four formal pre-higher-education selection points (entry to early-childhood education, primary school choice, age-10/12 gimnázium entry, and post-16 tracking), meaning reform of the 6/8-year track alone may have limited systemic effect on aggregate segregation without addressing capacity allocation and other junctures.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: This is an inference from the multiplicity of selection points, not a quantified counterfactual analysis.

No published study cited in the notes directly tests whether removing the 6/8-year track while leaving other selection points intact would reduce inequality; the claim is theoretical, based on system-design logic.
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
